We enjoyed fabulous service and a yummy meal on the pier at Scomo's, an iconic SF restaurant. First must mention the bread and whipped butter - I could live on this! Nevertheless went on to enjoy the clam chowder, creamy and delish, and my other half enjoyed his selection of raw oysters. We shared the mixed grill - shrimps, cod and scallops - tartare sauce and fries all perfectly cooked. The wait staff were helpful and fun. Food arrived promptly, drinks sorted. It was a most enjoyable meal.

This restaurant was superbly in atmosphere, food as well as the enjoyment of dining there. Our waiter was the "BEST" that I have experience at dining out. Solomon was all that you could asked for because he was polite, always had a smile on his face and said thank you when leaving our table throughout the numerous courses. You could see the gentleness in his eyes and how he interacted with my wife and I. Just a tremendous experience. Thank you for the experience of dining with you.

I am writing this review based on the experience of family members who were there.
Deplorable attention; the waiter, desperate for us to leave, was lifting our plates while we were still eating, brought us the bill without us asking for it, and did not give us the option to order coffee or dessert. Very unpleasant!! Terrible experience!!
